elements.sent-2411-030: Projection constants are essential tools in analyzing the geometric properties of normed spaces.
elements.sent-2411-031: For a Banach space $Y$ and a closed, complemented subspace $X\subset Y$, the projection constant $\lambda(X, Y) $ is defined as the infimum of the norms of all bounded linear projections from $Y$ onto $Y$.. Formally, \begin{equation}\lambda(Y, X) = \inf \big\{ \|P\| : \,\, P \in L(Y, X), \,\,\, P|_{X} = id_{X}\big\}\,.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-004: \begin{equation}\lambda(Y, X) = \inf \big\{ \|P\| : \,\, P \in L(Y, X), \,\,\, P|_{X} = id_{X}\big\}\,.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-032: The absolute projection constant for a Banach space $ X $ is given by \begin{equation}\lambda(X) := \sup \, \lambda(I(X),Y)\,,\end{equation} where the supremum is taken over all possible Banach spaces $ Y $ and isometric embeddings $ I \colon X \to Y $.
elements.sent-2411-033: In the case where $ X $ is a finite-dimensional Banach space, and $ X_1 $ is a subspace of a $ C(K) $-space that is isometrically isomorphic to $X$, we have (see, e.g., \cite[III.B.5 Theorem]{wojtaszczyk1996banach ): \begin{equation}\label{eq: l_infty inyective} \lambda(X) = \lambda(X_1, C(K))\,.\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-035: Projections are also of significant importance in approximation theory.

elements.section-2411-007: Non compact inclusions: Variants of the Ryll-Wojtaszczyk results
elements.sent-2411-278: The motivation for this section stems once again from a significant result in the remarkable work of Ryll and Wojtaszczyk \cite{ryll1983homogeneous .}
elements.sent-2411-279: In their study, the authors address the question posed by S.~Waigner regarding whether the identity map from $ H_\infty(B_{\ell_2^n ) $ to $ H_1(B_{\ell_2^n ) $ is compact for any positive integer $ n \ge 2 $.}}
elements.sent-2411-280: They demonstrate the existence of a~sequence $ (p_k)$ of $k$-homogeneous polynomials on the complex unit ball $ B_{\ell_2^n $ of $\mathbb{C ^n $ such that $ \|p_k\|_{\infty = 1$ and $ \|p_k\|_2 \geq \sqrt{\pi}\,2^{-n}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-281: It is noteworthy that the authors mention in \cite{ryll1983homogeneous that Waigner's question is closely connected to a well-known problem posed by Rudin in his monograph \cite{rudin1980 : Does there exist an inner function on the open unit ball of the Hilbert space $ \ell_2^n$ for $ n \ge 2$? For further details on this topic, see \cite{rudin1985 and \cite{wojtaszczyk1996banach .}}}}

elements.sent-2411-284: Now we are ready to prove the following theorem, which is a~general version of the Ryll-Wojtaszczyk result.
